name: Sequence Alignment — Needleman-Wunsch & Smith-Waterman
description: Dynamic programming for sequence alignment. Needleman-Wunsch (1970): global alignment of entire sequences; recurrence F(i,j) = max{F(i-1,j-1)+s, F(i-1,j)-g, F(i,j-1)-g}; traceback from (m,n). Smith-Waterman (1981): local alignment; set negative scores to 0; traceback from max cell until 0. Both O(mn) time and space. Scoring: match bonus, mismatch/gap penalties. Foundation for BLAST and alignment tools.
mermaid:
graph TD
    A1["Input: sequences S, T\nScoring: match, mismatch, gap"]
    B1["Initialize matrix F\nF(0,0)=0, F(i,0)=-ig, F(0,j)=-jg"]
    C1{"Global NW\nor Local SW?"}
    D1["NW: Fill F(i,j) = max of\nF(i-1,j-1)+s, F(i-1,j)-g, F(i,j-1)-g"]
    D2["SW: Same but F(i,j) = max(0, ...)\nNegative scores set to 0"]
    E1["Traceback: NW from (m,n)\nSW from argmax F, stop at 0"]
    F1["Output: alignment\nand score"]
    A1 --> B1
    B1 --> C1
    C1 -->|Global| D1
    C1 -->|Local| D2
    D1 --> E1
    D2 --> E1
    E1 --> F1
    classDef red fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    classDef yellow fill:#ffd43b,color:#000
    classDef green fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    classDef blue fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    class A1 red
    class B1,D1,D2 yellow
    class E1 green
    class F1 blue
